title: Stachydeoma graveolens
intro: Stachydeoma graveolens, also known as Himalayan Stachydeoma, is a perennial plant species native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its distinctive leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Stachydeoma graveolens include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Stachydeoma graveolens is often made into teas, infusions, or extracts, and is also used in the production of herbal syrups and cough drops.
